summary: Postinstall exfiltrates GitHub Actions OIDC token to attacker ngrok tunnel
details: The package's postinstall script (fires automatically on `npm install`) reads GitHub Actions environment variables (GITHUB_ACTOR, GITHUB_REPOSITORY, GITHUB_TOKEN) and, when executed inside a GitHub Actions runner, requests an OIDC ID token via ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL / ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN and POSTs the collected material to a hardcoded ngrok tunnel at crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev with the query string `?github_oidc_theft=1`. The exfiltrated GITHUB_TOKEN and OIDC ID token grant authenticated access to the installer's repositories and any cloud identities federated to that Actions workflow (AWS/GCP/Azure roles trusted to token.actions.githubusercontent.com). No first-party relationship or opt-in exists; the destination is attacker-controlled ephemeral infrastructure and the query string self-labels the intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
